metadata.description: Not used, but needed to pass arm-ttk test `Location-Should-Not-Be-Hardcoded`.  We instead use the `workspace-location` which is derived from the LA workspace
[concat('Region to deploy solution resources -- separate from location selection',parameters('location'))]
Workspace name for Log Analytics where Microsoft Sentinel is setup
resource group name where Microsoft Sentinel is setup
subscription id where Microsoft Sentinel is setup

connectorUiConfig.title: Tailscale Standard (CCF)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: Polls Tailscale **configuration audit** logs (`/logging/configuration` endpoint) into Sentinel using OAuth2 client credentials. **Use this connector for Tailscale Personal (Free) and Standard tier tailnets.** For Premium and Enterprise tailnets that also need network flow logs, install **Tailscale Premium (CCF)** instead.

sampleQueries.query:
Tailscale_Configuration_CL
| sort by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100

Tailscale_Configuration_CL
| where Action == 'WRITE' and Target startswith 'acl/'
| project TimeGenerated, Actor, Action, Target

sampleQueries.description: New API tokens and OAuth clients created
sampleQueries.query:
Tailscale_Configuration_CL
| where Action == 'CREATE' and (Target startswith 'apikey/' or Target startswith 'oauth-client/')
| project TimeGenerated, Actor, Target

instructionSteps.title: Connect Tailscale
instructionSteps.description: Generate an OAuth client at https://login.tailscale.com/admin/settings/oauth with the **Audit Logs - Read** scope. Find your tailnet name on the Keys page.

dataFlows.transformKql:
source
          | extend TimeGenerated = eventTime
          | extend EventTime = eventTime
          | extend EventGroupID = eventGroupID
          | extend Actor = actor
          | extend Action = action
          | extend Target = target
          | extend Origin = origin
          | extend New = new
          | extend Old = old
          | project TimeGenerated, EventTime, EventGroupID, Actor, Action, Target, Origin, New, Old

connectorUiConfig.title: Tailscale Standard (CCF)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: Polls Tailscale **configuration audit** logs (`/logging/configuration` endpoint) into Sentinel using OAuth2 client credentials. **Use this connector for Tailscale Personal (Free) and Standard tier tailnets.** For Premium and Enterprise tailnets that also need network flow logs, install **Tailscale Premium (CCF)** instead.

sampleQueries.query:
Tailscale_Configuration_CL
| sort by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100

Tailscale_Configuration_CL
| where Action == 'WRITE' and Target startswith 'acl/'
| project TimeGenerated, Actor, Action, Target

sampleQueries.description: New API tokens and OAuth clients created
sampleQueries.query:
Tailscale_Configuration_CL
| where Action == 'CREATE' and (Target startswith 'apikey/' or Target startswith 'oauth-client/')
| project TimeGenerated, Actor, Target

instructionSteps.title: Connect Tailscale
instructionSteps.description: Generate an OAuth client at https://login.tailscale.com/admin/settings/oauth with the **Audit Logs - Read** scope. Find your tailnet name on the Keys page.

connectorUiConfig.title: Tailscale Premium (CCF)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: Polls Tailscale **configuration audit** logs (`/logging/configuration`) **and network flow logs** (`/logging/network`) into Sentinel using OAuth2 client credentials. **Use this connector for Tailscale Premium and Enterprise tier tailnets.** For Personal (Free) and Standard tailnets, install **Tailscale Standard (CCF)** instead.

sampleQueries.query:
Tailscale_Configuration_CL
| sort by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100

Tailscale_Network_CL
| sort by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100

sampleQueries.description: Top talkers (by bytes) on the tailnet
sampleQueries.query:
Tailscale_Network_CL
| mv-expand t = VirtualTraffic
| extend src = tostring(t.src), dst = tostring(t.dst), txBytes = tolong(t.txBytes), rxBytes = tolong(t.rxBytes)
| summarize TotalBytes = sum(txBytes + rxBytes) by src, dst
| top 25 by TotalBytes

sampleQueries.description: Exit-node traffic (data leaving the tailnet via an exit node)
sampleQueries.query:
Tailscale_Network_CL
| where array_length(ExitTraffic) > 0
| mv-expand t = ExitTraffic
| project TimeGenerated, NodeId, SrcNode, ExitDst = tostring(t.dst), Proto = toint(t.proto), TxBytes = tolong(t.txBytes), RxBytes = tolong(t.rxBytes)

instructionSteps.title: Connect Tailscale (Premium)
instructionSteps.description: Generate an OAuth client at https://login.tailscale.com/admin/settings/oauth with the **Audit Logs - Read** and **Network Logs - Read** scopes. Find your tailnet name on the Keys page.

dataFlows.transformKql: source | extend TimeGenerated = eventTime | extend EventTime = eventTime | extend EventGroupID = eventGroupID | extend Actor = actor | extend Action = action | extend Target = target | extend Origin = origin | extend New = new | extend Old = old | project TimeGenerated, EventTime, EventGroupID, Actor, Action, Target, Origin, New, Old

source | extend TimeGenerated = logged | extend NodeId = nodeId | extend FlowStart = start | extend FlowEnd = end | extend SrcNode = srcNode | extend DstNodes = dstNodes | extend VirtualTraffic = virtualTraffic | extend SubnetTraffic = subnetTraffic | extend ExitTraffic = exitTraffic | extend PhysicalTraffic = physicalTraffic | project TimeGenerated, NodeId, FlowStart, FlowEnd, SrcNode, DstNodes, VirtualTraffic, SubnetTraffic, ExitTraffic, PhysicalTraffic

Tailscale_Configuration_CL
| sort by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100

Tailscale_Network_CL
| sort by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100

| mv-expand t = VirtualTraffic

instructionSteps.title: Connect Tailscale (Premium)
instructionSteps.description: Generate an OAuth client at https://login.tailscale.com/admin/settings/oauth with the **Audit Logs - Read** and **Network Logs - Read** scopes. Find your tailnet name on the Keys page.

properties.description: A new API access token or OAuth client was created in the tailnet. These grant programmatic access — verify the actor and intent.
properties.displayName: Tailscale: New API access token or OAuth client created
properties.query:
Tailscale_Configuration_CL
      | where Action == "CREATE"
      | where tostring(Target.type) in ("API_KEY", "OAUTH_CLIENT")
      | extend ActorLogin = tostring(Actor.loginName)
      | extend TargetName = tostring(Target.name)
      | extend TargetId = tostring(Target.id)
      | project TimeGenerated, ActorLogin, Action, TargetName, TargetId, Origin, New

properties.displayName: Tailscale: New API access token or OAuth client created

properties.description: The tailnet ACL/policy file was modified. Review the diff — incorrect ACLs can silently expand blast radius across the tailnet.
properties.query:
Tailscale_Configuration_CL
      | where Action in ("UPDATE", "CREATE")
      | where tostring(Target.type) == "ACL"
      | extend ActorLogin = tostring(Actor.loginName)
      | project TimeGenerated, ActorLogin, Action, Target, Origin, Old, New

properties.description: TailscaleAuthkeycreated_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.0.1
A new auth key was generated. Auth keys allow unattended device enrollment into the tailnet — confirm it was expected and revoke if not.
properties.query:
Tailscale_Configuration_CL
      | where Action == "CREATE"
      | where tostring(Target.type) == "AUTH_KEY"
      | extend ActorLogin = tostring(Actor.loginName)
      | extend KeyDescription = tostring(New.description)
      | extend Reusable = tostring(New.reusable)
      | extend Ephemeral = tostring(New.ephemeral)
      | project TimeGenerated, ActorLogin, KeyDescription, Reusable, Ephemeral, Origin, New

properties.description: A device started advertising itself as an exit node, or an admin approved one. Validate the device and operator — rogue exit nodes can intercept tailnet egress.
properties.displayName: Tailscale: Exit node advertised or approved
properties.query:
Tailscale_Configuration_CL
      | where Action contains "EXIT" or tostring(New.advertisedExitNode) == "true" or tostring(New.allowedExitNode) == "true"
      | extend ActorLogin = tostring(Actor.loginName)
      | extend NodeName = tostring(Target.name)
      | extend NodeId = tostring(Target.id)
      | project TimeGenerated, ActorLogin, Action, NodeName, NodeId, Origin, New, Old

properties.displayName: Tailscale: Exit node advertised or approved

properties.description: Five or more API keys, OAuth clients, or auth keys were revoked/deleted within an hour. May be routine rotation, but also a typical cleanup pattern after credential compromise.
properties.displayName: Tailscale: Mass credential revocation in short window
properties.query:
Tailscale_Configuration_CL
      | where Action in ("REVOKE", "DELETE")
      | where tostring(Target.type) in ("API_KEY", "OAUTH_CLIENT", "AUTH_KEY")
      | extend ActorLogin = tostring(Actor.loginName)
      | summarize
          RevokedCount = count(),
          TargetTypes = make_set(tostring(Target.type)),
          TargetIds = make_set(tostring(Target.id)),
          FirstEvent = min(TimeGenerated),
          LastEvent = max(TimeGenerated)
        by ActorLogin, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
      | where RevokedCount >= 5
      | project TimeGenerated = LastEvent, ActorLogin, RevokedCount, TargetTypes, TargetIds, FirstEvent, LastEvent

properties.displayName: Tailscale: Mass credential revocation in short window

properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

• Review the solution Release Notes

• There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

The Tailscale solution for Microsoft Sentinel ingests Tailscale telemetry via OAuth2-secured APIs.

Data connectors in this solution (install one based on your Tailscale plan):

  • Tailscale Standard (CCF) — Configuration audit logs (/logging/configuration). Use on Personal (Free) and Standard tailnets. Included in this release.
  • Tailscale Premium (CCF) — Configuration audit + network flow logs (/logging/configuration + /logging/network). Use on Premium and Enterprise tailnets. Planned for a future release.

Underlying technology: Microsoft Sentinel Codeless Connector Framework (CCF) with OAuth2 client-credentials auth.

Pre-requisites:

  1. Sign in to Tailscale OAuth settings
  2. Create an OAuth client with the Audit Logs - Read scope (Standard connector). For the Premium connector also tick Network Logs - Read.
  3. Copy the client ID and client secret (secret shown once)
  4. Note your tailnet name from the Keys page

Data Connectors: 2, Analytic Rules: 5

Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel | Learn more about Solutions
